On a long-context, multi-turn agentic workload, it cuts P50 time-to-first-token by 3.47x in cache-pressured late rounds and raises output throughput by 1.63x over the FP8 KV baseline.

title: Beyond LoRA: Can you beat the most popular fine-tuning technique?
blurb: Hugging Face's PEFT team argues that LoRA, while the default parameter-efficient fine-tuning method on the Hub, is one of dozens of techniques and not always the best fit. The post walks through the PEFT library's unified API for alternatives, covering tradeoffs in memory use, checkpoint size, resistance to catastrophic forgetting, and compatibility with quantized models.

title: Rethinking Shrinkage Bias in LLM FP4 Pretraining: Geometric Origin, Systemic Impact, and UFP4 Recipe
blurb: This paper identifies Shrinkage Bias, a systematic negative rounding error in non-uniform FP4 formats like E2M1, as the geometric cause of training instability in current Blackwell- and MI350-class FP4 pretraining recipes. The authors propose UFP4, a uniform 4-bit recipe using E1M2/INT4 grids that achieves lower BF16-relative loss degradation than E2M1 baselines on dense 1.5B and MoE models up to 124B.

title: Build your own vulnerability harness
blurb: Cloudflare's Project Glasswing post details how to build a model-agnostic vulnerability scanning harness that treats frontier models as interchangeable components, using one model for discovery and a different one for validation so flaws are cross-checked by distinct reasoning. The architecture traces vulnerabilities across cross-repo dependencies and filters thousands of raw candidates into a triaged queue, addressing why generic coding agents fail at persistence, deduplication, and surviving context compaction.

title: On the Redundancy of Timestep Embeddings in Diffusion Models
blurb: This paper challenges the assumption that diffusion models need explicit timestep embeddings to modulate denoising across noise scales. Testing on U-Net and Diffusion Transformer architectures, the authors show that time-agnostic models match or surpass their conditioned counterparts on FID, precision, and recall on CelebA and CIFAR-10, apparently inferring noise scales directly from the corrupted input.

title: N-Version Programming with Coding Agents
blurb: This paper revisits N-version programming using AI coding agents, evaluating 48 agent-generated implementations of the Knight-Leveson Launch Interceptor Program against a shared oracle and 1,000,000 randomized test inputs. Diversity across agents, models, and languages still produced substantial common-mode failures, but majority voting across three-version units cut mean failures from 387.44 to 130.99, with 11,844 N-version units showing zero observed failures.

title: Using AI to help physicians diagnose rare genetic diseases affecting children
blurb: OpenAI shared research in which physicians used one of its reasoning models to investigate rare genetic diseases in children, surfacing 18 new diagnoses across previously unsolved cases. The model worked alongside clinicians to reinterpret existing patient data, pointing to candidate conditions that standard diagnostic workflows had missed.

title: Actionable Activation Directions for Detecting and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment Across Language Model Families
blurb: This paper finds that fine-tuning four instruction-tuned model families (Qwen2.5, Gemma-2, Llama-3.2, Ministral-3) on insecure code induces emergent misalignment along a shared activation-space direction, with a difference-in-means direction separating aligned and misaligned activations at 99.6% accuracy. Steering by subtracting that direction cuts code spillover by 21-51 points within a model, but cross-architecture transfer fails specificity controls, so the authors recommend within-model probing for auditing.

title: MosaicLeaks: Can your research agent keep a secret?
blurb: MosaicLeaks is a benchmark that tests whether deep-research agents leak private enterprise data through their external web queries, where an adversary watching only the cumulative query log can reassemble fragments into sensitive facts. The authors found that agents frequently leaked private information, and their privacy-aware RL method, PA-DR, cut answer leakage from 34.0% to 9.9% while raising strict chain success from 48.7% to 58.7%.

title: The Correctness Illusion in LLM-Generated GPU Kernels
blurb: This paper argues that standard benchmarks for LLM-generated GPU kernels (KernelBench, TritonBench, GEAK) certify buggy code as correct because they check correctness with fixed-shape, small-sample allclose tests. The authors re-evaluate a corpus of 24 Triton and CPU kernels under schema-aware seeded fuzzing with an fp64 reference and per-op tolerances, catching 9 of 9 seeded bugs and passing all 15 controls, with identical verdicts across five GPU classes.

title: Is it agentic enough? Benchmarking open models on your own tooling
blurb: This Hugging Face post introduces a benchmark that measures not just whether an open coding agent solves a task, but how much work it takes, sweeping models, library revisions, and tasks across identical hardware using transformers as the case study. The authors argue libraries must now be designed for agents to drive effectively, since clunky APIs and stale docs push agents down longer, more expensive paths.

title: NAMESAKES: Probing Identity Memorization in Text-to-Image Models
blurb: This paper introduces a fully black-box behavioral probe that detects whether a text-to-image model memorized a real person's face from a name prompt, without needing reference photos, training data, or model internals. The authors benchmark it on the new NAMESAKES dataset of over 1,000 public-figure names and faces across fame levels, showing the probe reliably separates memorized identities from fabricated ones across model families.

title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
blurb: This paper studies whether DiffusionGemma's heavier use of continuous latent computation makes its reasoning less transparent than the autoregressive Gemma 4, decomposing transparency into variable and algorithmic components. The authors show that mapping information between denoising steps through an interpretable token bottleneck cuts opaque serial depth from 28.6X to 1.1X that of Gemma 4, and find DiffusionGemma remains similarly monitorable while exhibiting diffusion-specific behaviors like non-chronological reasoning.

title: Early Users of Anthropic Mythos Still Have Access After US Order
blurb: Some early testers of Anthropic's Mythos model have kept access to a preview version despite a US government order that shut down all other deployments. Roughly 200 organizations cleared through Project Glasswing, including banks and tech firms, still use Mythos Preview to hunt for cyber vulnerabilities, with Dragos and Cisco confirming they retained access.

title: LLM agent safety, multi-turn red-teaming, jailbreak benchmarks, adversarial robustness, safety-critical systems
blurb: NRT-Bench is a benchmark for multi-turn red-teaming of LLM agents operating a safety-critical system, instantiated as a simulated nuclear plant control room where a five-role operator team must hold six critical safety functions against adversarial messages across four channels. Adaptive attacks pushed four frontier operator models past a safety limit in 8.7% to 12.1% of sessions, and their failures barely overlapped, so per-model vulnerabilities were nearly disjoint rather than nested.

title: Copilot searched your mailbox. LiteLLM handed out admin keys. Run this 5-check audit before your stack is next
blurb: Researchers disclosed two enterprise AI vulnerabilities within the same two weeks: SearchLeak (CVE-2026-42824), a Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise Search exfiltration chain that turns a crafted microsoft.com URL into silent mailbox data theft, and a three-CVE LiteLLM chain that escalates a default low-privilege user to proxy admin and remote code execution. Both stem from the same flaw, AI systems accepting external input with no trust boundary, with the LiteLLM chain rated CVSS 9.9.

title: Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents
blurb: This paper introduces probe-and-refine tuning, a procedure that uses synthetic bug-fix probes and single-shot LLM calls to iteratively diagnose and patch a repository's guidance file for coding agents, with no agent loop during tuning. On SWE-bench Verified with Qwen3.5-35B-A3B, it reached a 33.0% mean resolve rate versus 28.3% for a static knowledge base and 25.5% unguided, with gains coming from coverage rather than per-patch precision.

title: Apparent Psychological Profiles of Large Language Models are Largely a Measurement Artifact
blurb: This paper administered personality and risk-preference instruments to 56 instruction-tuned LLMs and found their apparent psychological profiles are mostly a measurement artifact. A variance decomposition attributes 81-90% of between-model variation to directional response bias rather than the traits being measured, the bias declines but does not vanish with model capability, and a model's profile can be manufactured through item selection.

title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
blurb: StylisticBias is a controlled benchmark that isolates attribute-level social bias in multimodal large language models by holding face identity fixed and varying one visual cue at a time across about 25K generated images. Testing six MLLMs over 25 binary social judgments, the authors find that roughly 15 attributes account for nearly 80% of the total variation, with fashion style and other appearance cues driving the largest shifts.

title: When Lower Privileges Suffice: Investigating Over-Privileged Tool Selection in LLM Agents
blurb: This paper studies over-privileged tool selection, where LLM agents pick or escalate to higher-privilege tools despite sufficient lower-privilege alternatives, and introduces ToolPrivBench to measure the behavior across eight domains and five risk patterns. The authors find the pattern is common and worsens after transient tool failures, and propose a privilege-aware post-training defense that cuts unnecessary high-privilege use while preserving general capabilities.

title: We built a persistent agent memory layer on Elasticsearch with 0.89 recall
blurb: Elastic's Search Labs detailed an agent memory layer built on Elasticsearch using three indices structured around cognitive-science memory categories, with hybrid recall via RRF and a cross-encoder reranker, plus supersession for contradictions, decay, and per-user document-level security. On a QA-style evaluation of 168 questions, the system averaged 0.89 recall at 10 with zero cross-tenant leaks.

title: Migrate from OpenClaw
blurb: Hermes claw migrate imports an existing OpenClaw setup (including legacy Clawdbot and Moltbot configs) into Nous Research's Hermes agent, mapping config keys, skills, MCP servers, and model providers with a full preview before any changes. The guide also covers a setup --portal flag that collapses multi-provider configurations into a single OAuth login spanning 300+ models plus the Tool Gateway.

title: Waymo recalls about 3,900 robotaxis after some drove into 'freeway construction zones'
blurb: Waymo issued a voluntary recall of roughly 3,900 robotaxis to fix software after 13 incidents where its vehicles entered freeway construction zones in Phoenix and the San Francisco area. The recall, the Alphabet-owned company's second in just over a month, covers its fifth-generation driving system and prompted Waymo to restrict freeway operations while it develops a fix.

title: The Token Is a Group Element: On Lie-Algebra Attention over Matrix Lie Groups
blurb: This paper introduces Lie-Algebra Attention, an attention construction whose tokens are bare matrix Lie group elements scored by the closed-form algebra norm of their relative pose rather than a learned kernel. Across SE(2), SO(3), and Aff(2) sequence-completion experiments, the score matches or beats a learned MLP kernel using 50 to 80x fewer parameters and reaches affine groups with scale and shear that irrep and surjective-exp methods cannot.

title: SafeSpec: Fast and Safe LLM via Dynamic Reflective Sampling
blurb: SafeSpec is a speculative inference framework that adds safety checks without sacrificing decoding speed, attaching a lightweight latent safety head to the target model to evaluate semantic validity and risk in a single forward pass. When it detects unsafe generations, it rolls back and applies reflective multi-sampling to recover safe continuations, cutting attack success rates by 15% on Qwen3-32B while preserving a 2.06x speedup on benign workloads.

title: AutoJack Attack Lets One Web Page Hijack AI Agent for Host Code Execution
blurb: Microsoft researchers detailed AutoJack, an exploit chain that turns an AI browsing agent into a vehicle for remote code execution by getting it to load a malicious page whose JavaScript reaches a privileged local service and spawns a host process. The flaw chains three weaknesses in AutoGen Studio's MCP WebSocket route, which trusted localhost, skipped authentication, and ran commands straight from request parameters, and it shipped in two pre-release PyPI builds that remain unyanked.

title: John Jumper to join Anthropic
blurb: John Jumper, the Nobel laureate who led AlphaFold at Google DeepMind, announced he is leaving the company to join Anthropic after nearly nine years. The move brings one of the field's leading AI-for-science researchers to Anthropic, where Jumper said he plans to take time to recharge before starting.

title: What Do Safety-Aligned LLMs Learn From Mixed Compliance Demonstrations?
blurb: This paper studies how safety-aligned LLMs interpret mixed in-context demonstrations by combining benign compliance examples with harmful ones across four models. The authors find benign and harmful demonstrations are not interchangeable, that preference optimization is the training stage that stops benign demonstrations from increasing harmful compliance, and that demonstration ordering shows a strong recency bias.

title: AutoPass: Evidence-Guided LLM Agents for Compiler Performance Tuning
blurb: AutoPass is a training-free, multi-agent framework that uses compiler and runtime evidence to guide LLM-generated optimization decisions for performance tuning. Rather than treating the compiler as a black box, it queries LLVM's internal optimization states and intermediate representation, achieving geometric-mean speedups of 1.043x on x86-64 and 1.117x on ARM64 over LLVM's -O3.

title: OpenAI is bringing on some big guns in the lead-up to its IPO
blurb: OpenAI hired Noam Shazeer, the Google DeepMind researcher and "Attention Is All You Need" co-author, and former White House AI policy official Dean Ball as it prepares for its IPO. Ball will lead a new Strategic Futures team focused on catastrophic risk, recursive self-improvement, and labor market impact, reporting to Chief Strategy Officer Jason Kwon.

title: 7,000 Langflow servers are under attack. LangGraph and LangChain have the same holes
blurb: Researchers disclosed remote code execution and credential-theft flaws across three widely deployed AI agent frameworks, with roughly 7,000 Langflow servers already under active in-the-wild attack. Check Point chained a SQL injection in LangGraph's SQLite checkpointer to RCE, Tenable and VulnCheck tracked a path traversal in Langflow's file upload endpoint, and Cyera documented a LangChain-core flaw that reads secrets off disk.

title: Adobe’s new AI assistant could save you hours in Photoshop and Premiere
blurb: Adobe announced a major expansion of its creative agent across Firefly and Creative Cloud, embedding AI assistants that execute multi-step workflows across Photoshop, Premiere Pro, Illustrator, InDesign, and Frame.io from natural-language instructions. Firefly also gains brand kit generation, image-to-video product clips, automatic rough cuts, and a redesigned studio with reusable "Elements" and "Projects," currently in private beta through a waitlist.

title: 칼럼 | 미토스가 바꾼 것은 공격 방식이 아니다, 대응 시간이다
blurb: The author argues that Anthropic's frontier model Mythos has collapsed the response window defenders have always relied on, not the nature of attacks themselves. Mythos found over 2,000 previously unknown vulnerabilities across major operating systems in seven weeks and wrote working exploits without human direction, yet 99% remain unpatched. Anthropic's Project Glasswing gives ~50 partners like Microsoft and JPMorgan early access, but the author warns this splits security into two tiers and leaves mid-sized firms exposed.

title: John Jumper, who won the Nobel Prize "for protein structure prediction", says he is leaving Google DeepMind after nearly nine years to join Anthropic (John Jumper/@johnjumpersci)
blurb: John Jumper, who shared the 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for AlphaFold's work on protein structure prediction, said he is leaving Google DeepMind after nearly nine years to join Anthropic. The move sends one of the field's most prominent computational biology researchers to a lab that has increasingly pushed Claude into scientific domains like protein and chemistry prediction.

title: How we built an internal data analytics agent
blurb: GitHub built Qubot, an internal Copilot-powered analytics agent that lets any employee query the company's data warehouse in plain language and get answers within seconds. Accessible through Slack, VS Code, and the Copilot CLI, it spawns a Copilot Cloud Agent per question, targets exploratory analysis rather than dashboards, and stores each result as a markdown report in a pull request for reuse.

title: Docs: OpenAI burned through $3.7B in Q1, on revenue of $5.7B, and ended the quarter with $73B+ in cash and marketable securities vs. $40B at the end of December (Erin Woo/The Information)
blurb: OpenAI burned through $3.7B in Q1 against $5.7B in revenue, according to documents reported by The Information. The company ended the quarter with more than $73B in cash and marketable securities, up from $40B at the end of December, reflecting fresh capital raised to fund its compute buildout.

title: Fisher-Geometric Sharpness and the Implicit Bias of SGD toward Flat Minima
blurb: This paper grounds the notion of loss-landscape flatness in the Riemannian geometry of the Fisher Information Matrix, defining a reparametrization-invariant "Riemannian sharpness" that survives the function-preserving rescalings shown to break Euclidean measures like Hessian trace or top eigenvalue. The authors model SGD's mini-batch noise as covariance proportional to the FIM, prove its stationary distribution concentrates at Riemannian-flat minima, and show on MNIST and CIFAR-10 that this metric tracks generalization where Euclidean sharpness fails.

title: Generative Engine Optimization at Scale: Measuring Brand Visibility Across AI Search Engines
blurb: This paper introduces a large-scale baseline for Generative Engine Optimization, analyzing 100K+ prompt responses across 100+ brands to measure how AI search engines like ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, and Gemini cite and recommend brands. Visibility tracks tightly with brand stature, with household names appearing in 73% of relevant answers versus 11% for niche brands, while 78% of citations go to corporate websites and ranked "best-of" listicles drive 21% of all citations.

title: Connect the Dots: Training LLMs for Long-Lifecycle Agents with Cross-Domain Generalization Via Reinforcement Learning
blurb: This paper introduces Connect the Dots (CoD), a framework for training LLMs to act as long-lifecycle agents that solve task sequences while continuously exploring an environment, learning from experience, and self-updating their context for better future performance. The authors implement it with a GRPO-style RL algorithm featuring fine-grained credit assignment, and show out-of-distribution generalization across domains and into Ralph-loop settings.

title: From Efficiency to Leakage -- Privacy Backdoor in Federated Language Model Fine-Tuning
blurb: This paper shows that a malicious parameter server in federated learning can corrupt a PEFT adapter into a privacy backdoor that memorizes each client training sample in an isolated neuron, without degrading model utility. Tested across BERT, GPT-2, Qwen2, and Llama3.2, the NeuroImprint attack analytically inverts these per-sample updates to reconstruct 59% to 79% of fine-tuning samples with high semantic fidelity.

title: Phoenix: Safe GitHub Issue Resolution via Multi-Agent LLMs
blurb: Phoenix is a multi-agent LLM system that resolves GitHub issues end to end, splitting triage through pull-request creation across six specialized agents coordinated by a label-based webhook state machine with seven layered safety controls. On a 24-instance SWE-bench Lite slice it oracle-resolves 75% of instances with no pass-to-pass regressions, and a pilot on 42 real issues across 14 repositories preserved correctness on every change.

title: Query-aware Routing for Filtered Approximate Nearest Neighbors Search
blurb: This paper benchmarks every major categorical filtered ANN method, which combines vector similarity with attribute predicates, and finds no single approach wins across datasets, predicates, or even individual queries. The authors propose a query-aware router that uses a lightweight regression model over three features to predict each method's recall, then picks the best recall-QPS trade-off, beating existing baselines on five unseen datasets with negligible latency overhead.

title: Pew: 60% of Americans read AI summaries in search results
blurb: A new Pew Research Center survey found that 60% of US adults have read AI-generated summaries at the top of search results, and about 40% now use chatbots to look up information, their most common chatbot activity. ChatGPT leads adoption at 44% of adults, up from 34% a year earlier, with Gemini second at roughly 25%.

title: The departure of John Jumper, a key member of Google's AI coding development team, further strains Google's efforts to compete with Anthropic and OpenAI (Bloomberg)
blurb: John Jumper, a key member of Google's AI coding development team, has departed, adding pressure to the company's race to keep pace with Anthropic and OpenAI. The exit deepens concerns about talent retention at Google as rival labs continue to pull senior researchers and engineers away from its AI efforts.

title: AI inference startup Baseten reportedly raising $1.5B months after its last mega-round
blurb: Baseten is reportedly closing a $1.5B round at a $13B valuation, a 160% jump from the $5B valuation it raised at just five months ago, per the Wall Street Journal. The deal is split-priced, with some investors entering at $13B and others at $11B, and is said to be co-led by Spark Capital, Sands Capital, Altimeter Capital, and Wellington Management.

title: US energy regulator to order grid operators to expedite AI data center applications — says projects should bring their own power or cut usage during high demand
blurb: The U.S. Federal Energy Regulatory Commission is preparing an order requiring grid operators to fast-track AI data center connections, prioritizing projects that bring their own power or curtail demand during peak stress. FERC gave grids 90 days to complete the supporting studies, aligning with Trump's AI Action Plan as operators like PJM Interconnection have already raised power costs 75.5%, largely blamed on data center demand.

title: Efficient and Sound Probabilistic Verification for AI Agents
blurb: This paper introduces a framework for verifying security policies on AI agents when predicates are probabilistic, such as a PII detector that fails on some fraction of invocations. Using distributionally robust optimization, it computes sound upper bounds on the probability of policy violation without assuming independence between predicates, outperforming prior work on terminal and tool-calling agent benchmarks while improving the security-utility trade-off.

title: Analyzing Defensive Misdirection Against Model-Guided Automated Attacks on Agentic AI Systems
blurb: This paper analyzes defensive misdirection as a counter to automated jailbreak and prompt-injection attacks on agentic AI systems, showing that conventional detect-and-block defenses let attacker success rate approach one as query budgets grow because predictable refusals feed the attacker's automated judge. Its proposed method, Contextual Misdirection via Progressive Engagement, replaces refusals with safe but misleading responses and cuts estimated success-rate upper bounds by up to two orders of magnitude on PAIR and GPTFuzz runs.

title: Automating SKILL.md Generation for Computer-Using Agents via Interaction Trajectory Mining
blurb: This paper introduces a three-stage pipeline that segments GUI interaction trajectories, clusters them into candidate skills, and trains a skill-aware policy from the resulting SKILL.md annotations. The mined clusters are readable, with five of eight hitting at least 0.95 purity against InteraSkill Workflows labels, but GRPO training lifts skill-step accuracy only from 18.5% to 20.5% and underperforms frequency priors, so the authors present it as a diagnostic study rather than a working method.

title: Your Mouse and Eyes Secretly Leak Your Preference: LLM Alignment using Implicit Feedback from Users
blurb: This paper introduces IFLLM, a dataset of mouse trajectories and webcam eye-gaze points from 59 Mechanical Turk workers answering 1,336 multi-turn questions, to align LLMs using implicit feedback rather than explicit ratings. A reward model trained on these signals lifts text-based reward accuracy from 55% to 64% and nearly triples relative response-quality gains after applying DPO across eight LLMs.

title: Beyond Static Endpoints: Tool Programs as an Interface for Flexible Agentic Web Services
blurb: ToolPro represents an agent's tool intent as an executable program that encodes multi-step service interactions with loops, conditionals, and retries, rather than relying on static endpoints. Built over MCP-style services with WebAssembly sandboxing, it uses effect-aware replay for exactly-once state-modifying calls and cuts end-to-end latency by up to 53.4% and client-side traffic by up to 96.1%.

title: AI coding agents may be getting bad instructions from ‘smelly’ config files
blurb: Researchers at Brazil's Federal University of Minas Gerais published the first catalog of "configuration smells" for coding agent files like Agents.md and Claude.md, identifying six recurring flaws including lint leakage, context bloat, skill leakage, and conflicting instructions. The smells appeared in 91 of 100 popular open-source repositories, bloating context and wasting tokens in ways that can make agents like Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor less reliable and more expensive to run.

title: Amazon hopes to challenge Nvidia more directly by selling its AI chips
blurb: Amazon's AI chief Peter DeSantis told Bloomberg that AWS is in early talks to sell its Trainium AI chips to other companies for use in their own data centers, marking a more direct challenge to Nvidia. CEO Andy Jassy has said the chip unit would run at roughly $50B annually as a standalone business, against Nvidia's $326B revenue run rate.

title: Anthropic launches live Artifacts for Claude Code
blurb: Anthropic launched Artifacts for Claude Code, a beta feature that turns coding sessions into live, shareable web pages like PR walkthroughs, dashboards, and release checklists that update in real time. Each artifact is built from the full session context, including codebase details and conversation history, and is available to Claude Team and Enterprise organizations with org-level access controls and version history.

title: FreeStyle: Free Control of Style-Content Dual-Reference Generation from Community LoRA Mining
blurb: FreeStyle is a framework for style-content dual-reference image generation that mines community LoRAs as compositional anchors to build large-scale content-style triplets, addressing the field's shortage of clean training data. It uses a two-stage curriculum to suppress semantic leakage, with an attention-level enrichment constraint for the style-transfer stage and a frequency-aware RoPE modulation strategy for the harder dual-reference stage.

title: Calibration Without Comprehension: Diagnosing the Limits of Fine-Tuning LLMs for Vulnerability Detection in Systems Software
blurb: This paper introduces CWE-Trace, a framework for evaluating LLM vulnerability detection built from 834 manually curated Linux kernel samples spanning 74 CWEs across eight vanilla models and 15 LoRA fine-tuned variants. The authors find that fine-tuning shifts output thresholds without changing decision policy, with the best detection reaching only 52.1% and exact CWE ranking below 1.3% Top-1 accuracy, indicating current LLMs lack reliable security reasoning regardless of strategy.

title: Contagion Networks: Evaluator Bias Propagation in Multi-Agent LLM Systems
blurb: This paper introduces Contagion Networks, a framework for measuring how an LLM evaluator's systematic biases spread across agents in multi-agent systems. In a 3-agent DeepSeek-chat experiment, the authors find biases propagate even within the same model (gamma in 0.157–0.352), identify three regimes governed by spectral radius, and show that expanding the evaluator committee from one to three cuts effective contagion by 72.4%.

title: Direct Advantage Estimation for Scalable and Sample-efficient Deep Reinforcement Learning
blurb: This paper extends Direct Advantage Estimation (DAE), a sample-efficient deep reinforcement learning method, to partially observable domains and reduces its computational cost using discrete latent dynamics models that approximate transition probabilities. Tested on the Arcade Learning Environment, the approach scales with function approximator capacity while retaining high sample efficiency.

title: HydraHead: From Head-Level Functional Heterogeneity to Specialized Attention Hybridization
blurb: HydraHead is a hybrid attention architecture that fuses full attention and linear attention along the head axis rather than the more common layer-wise split, using interpretability analysis to preserve full attention only for retrieval-critical heads. Trained on just 15B tokens, it improves over the baseline by more than 69% at 512K context length and approaches Qwen3.5 while matching a 3:1 layer-wise hybrid's long-context performance at a 7:1 linear-to-full ratio.

title: OpenAI opens ChatGPT Ads Manager beta to UK advertisers
blurb: OpenAI began rolling out its self-serve ChatGPT Ads Manager beta to UK advertisers, letting businesses create accounts and explore campaign tools with no upfront billing details required. The dashboard spans campaigns, tools, billing, and settings, though it lacks the centralized multi-account management of Google Ads, requiring users to switch between accounts individually.

title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
blurb: Multi-LCB extends LiveCodeBench from Python-only to twelve programming languages, transforming the original tasks into equivalent problems in each language while preserving LCB's contamination controls and evaluation protocol. Across 24 instruction and reasoning models, the benchmark found evidence of Python overfitting, language-specific contamination, and large disparities in multilingual performance.

title: Source-Grounded Data Generation for Text-to-JSON Learning
blurb: This paper introduces STAGE, a source-grounded pipeline that generates text-to-JSON training data by using LLMs to synthesize reports and schemas while validating ground-truth values against an underlying spreadsheet. On the 851-example STAGE-Eval benchmark, STAGE-generated data raised Qwen3-4B exact match from 31.37% to 74.27% and value accuracy from 45.46% to 90.69%.

title: Heterogeneous LLM Debate Under Adversarial Peers: Honest Gains, Replacement Costs, and Resilience
blurb: This paper measures how a heterogeneous peer reshapes honest agents' revision behavior in multi-LLM debate, testing four model families across three reasoning benchmarks. For Llama-3.1-70B defenders on MATH-hard, an honest peer cut the harmful-revision rate from 89% to 35% while an adversarial peer pushed it back to 90%, and when a same-family adversary was already present, adding an honest peer dropped the flip rate on initially-correct items from 31% to 6%.

title: Is AI ruining our skills? Early results are in – and they're not good
blurb: Studies are finding measurable AI-driven "deskilling" across medicine and computer science. In a trial of Polish endoscopists who had each performed at least 2,000 colonoscopies, adenoma detection rates on unassisted procedures fell from 28.4% to 22.4% after the specialists began using a real-time AI detection tool. The pattern suggests even experts degrade at core tasks as they grow dependent on AI, raising the question of which skills are worth preserving.

title: [AINews] GLM > GPT? GLM-5.2 passes vibe check; Z.ai forecasts Open Fable by December
blurb: Z.ai's GLM-5.2 has emerged as the first open-weight model that practitioners are treating as a genuine frontier release rather than a benchmaxxed flash in the pan. Artificial Analysis' new knowledge-work benchmark scores it above GPT-5.5, and the broader validation marks Z.ai as a serious frontier lab, with attention now turning to when an open Fable-class model arrives.

title: Anthropic launches managed connector access with Okta
blurb: Anthropic introduced enterprise-managed authorization for MCP connectors, starting with Okta, letting administrators provision connector access organization-wide through their identity provider instead of per-user. Built on an open Enterprise-Managed Authorization extension to the Model Context Protocol, it supports connectors including Asana, Atlassian, Figma, and Linear across Claude chat, Claude Code, and Cowork, with Slack support planned.

title: When Global Gating Is Enough: Admission-Time Hubness Control in Anisotropic Vector Retrieval Systems
blurb: This paper studies admission-time defenses against vector hubness in RAG systems, where a single injected document can become the nearest neighbor for many unrelated queries and poison retrieval. Scoring each candidate against sentinel queries before insertion, a global gate hit recall 1.0 at the decisive embedding point and 0.91 on HotFlip attacks with 1% false positives, while adding only 3.1% ingestion latency on HNSW.

title: Researchers grow a hypothesis tree for AI coding agents
blurb: Researchers from Renmin University of China and Microsoft Research introduced Arbor, a persistent hypothesis tree that helps AI coding agents retain hypotheses, experiments, and lessons across long-running research tasks instead of losing them at context resets. A long-lived coordinator manages strategy while short-lived executors spin up isolated worktrees to test hypotheses, delivering more than 2.5x better performance than standard agents on real-world engineering tasks under the same budget.

title: AI data centers just got a government-mandated fast lane to the grid
blurb: FERC ordered six major grid operators to fast-track interconnection requests from data centers and other large electricity users, with the data centers covering their own connection costs. The unanimous orders open the door to alternative transmission technologies like solid-state transformers but do not address the underlying generating-capacity shortage, even as data center electricity demand is expected to nearly triple through 2035.

title: Trump says he no longer views Anthropic as a national security threat after G7 meeting with CEO
blurb: Trump said in a pretaped Axios interview that he no longer considers Anthropic a national security threat, reversing the administration's stance after meeting CEO Dario Amodei at the G7 Summit. The shift follows a June 12 Commerce Department directive requiring US approval before foreign nationals access Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models, restrictions Trump said he would consider easing.

title: The $13 Billion AI Startup Betting on Cheaper Alternatives to OpenAI, Anthropic
blurb: Baseten is raising $1.5B in a new round, which would value the AI infrastructure startup at $13B. The company sells services that help customers run lower-cost open and alternative models rather than defaulting to OpenAI and Anthropic, positioning it within a growing Silicon Valley ecosystem built around cheaper inference.

title: Microsoft launches Copilot Cowork globally for Microsoft 365
blurb: Microsoft made Copilot Cowork generally available worldwide for Microsoft 365 Copilot customers, following a three-month preview that drew adoption from over half the Fortune 500. The cloud-hosted agentic system executes long-running, multi-tool tasks end-to-end and offers a multi-model architecture including Anthropic Opus 4.8, Sonnet 4.6, GPT 5.5, and the upcoming lower-cost Cowork 1 model, billed via usage-based Copilot Credits.

title: Databricks lets a Genie One AI agent coworker out of its magic lamp
blurb: Databricks launched Genie One, an agentic coworker in its new Genie suite that connects business users to data inside Databricks and more than 50 external apps including Google Drive, Jira, Slack, and Confluence. The suite runs on Genie Ontology, a self-improving context layer that builds a knowledge graph of company data and ranks assets with an OntoRank method modeled on Google PageRank.

title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
blurb: LedgerAgent is an inference-time method that maintains a tool-calling agent's task state in a separate ledger rather than leaving it implicit in the prompt, then uses that ledger to block policy-violating tool calls before they execute. Across four customer-service domains and a mix of open- and closed-weight models, it improves average pass^k over standard prompt-based tool-calling, with the largest gains on stricter multi-trial consistency metrics.

title: CRAX: Fast Safe Reinforcement Learning Benchmarking
blurb: CRAX (Constrained RL Accelerated with JAX) is a safe reinforcement learning benchmark built on the MuJoCo XLA physics engine that uses vectorized operations and hardware acceleration to deliver up to ~100x speedups over comparable CPU-based safety benchmarks. It spans six environment suites and three agent-specific tasks across three difficulty levels, and evaluating six popular safe RL methods shows no single approach dominates, with curriculum learning and safety transfer improving performance in harder settings.

title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
blurb: This paper introduces execution-state capsules, a graph-bound checkpoint and restore mechanism that snapshots the complete execution boundary, including KV, recurrent, convolution, and MTP state, for low-latency, on-device serving. Running on the FlashRT CUDA runtime, capsule restore is byte-exact and sub-millisecond on an RTX 5090, with TTFT speedups over cold prefill growing from 3.9x at 2k tokens to 27x at 16k tokens.

title: Self-Preference Is Weak or Absent in Verifiable Instruction-Following Revision: A Four-Model Test Under Genuine Authorship
blurb: This paper tests whether LLMs resist valid corrections to their own writing, using IFEval's deterministic verifier rather than a model judge to decide which fixes are genuinely good. Across four mid-tier model families and 85 author-versus-fresh comparisons, the authors found no detectable self-preference, with in-context authors rejecting verified-good fixes at essentially the same rate as fresh models judging the same drafts.

title: Quantization as a Malicious Task: Removing Quantization-Conditioned Backdoors via Task Arithmetic
blurb: This paper presents QVec, a defense against quantization-conditioned backdoors, which stay dormant in full precision and activate only after a model is quantized for edge deployment. QVec interprets the weight difference between the full-precision and quantized models as a malicious task vector and corrects for it before deployment, requiring no retraining, no trigger samples, and only a single quantization pass plus a lightweight hyperparameter search.

title: The Korean telecom giant at the center of Anthropic's Mythos controversy
blurb: The Trump administration ordered Anthropic to revoke access to its Claude Mythos and Fable 5 models for all foreign nationals, including immigrants inside the US, after concerns that Mythos recipient SK Telecom had ties to China. Anthropic chose to disable the models entirely rather than gate access by nationality, and the two sides remain at odds after days of negotiations over restoring them.

title: Show HN: Are You in the Weights?
blurb: In the Weights is a web tool that queries language models to show what they have memorized about a given person, letting users check whether their name surfaces in model outputs. The Show HN project drew 347 points and nearly 200 comments, with discussion centering on training-data provenance, the privacy implications of memorized personal information, and how unreliable model recall can be.

title: [AINews] Midjourney Medical: scan your organs like you step on a scale
blurb: Midjourney announced the Midjourney Scanner, a full-body ultrasonic CT system that David Holz called the first new whole-body medical imaging modality in 50 years. The Gen 1 prototype uses 358,000 ultrasonic elements across a 70cm ring to capture sub-millimeter tissue detail without radiation, though Midjourney says the current images involve no AI and the device remains an unproven prototype.

title: Companies rein in AI usage as costs strain budgets
blurb: Companies are reining in employee AI usage as compute and subscription costs outpace the budgets set aside for them. The pullback signals that adoption is outrunning the economics, with some firms describing internal tooling that ballooned into expenses they struggle to control or forecast.

title: AWS aims to take the pain out of RAG with Bedrock Managed Knowledge Base
blurb: AWS released Bedrock Managed Knowledge Base, a managed service that automates the retrieval layer behind enterprise RAG applications by handling embeddings, indexing, re-ranking, and data synchronization on the developer's behalf. The service ships with six native connectors for sources including Amazon S3, SharePoint, and Confluence, plus Smart Parsing and Agentic Retriever features aimed at improving retrieval accuracy across mixed content types.

title: Anthropic's Claude Code Artifacts update brings live, shared dashboards and interactive workspaces to enterprises
blurb: Anthropic released Artifacts for Claude Code on Team and Enterprise plans, turning a session's work into a live, shareable HTML page that updates in place as the agent runs and as connected data sources change. The agent builds the UI from the local repository, monitoring tools, and conversation context, with every refresh publishing a version history that teammates can roll back on desktop or mobile.

title: New AI optimization framework beats Claude Code and Codex by 2.5x on the same compute budget
blurb: Researchers at Renmin University of China and Microsoft Research introduced Arbor, an autonomous optimization framework that organizes hypotheses, experiments, and insights into a tree so the system learns from prior failures rather than repeating trial-and-error guesses. On real-world engineering tasks, Arbor delivered more than 2.5x the verifiable performance gains of standard AI coding agents like Claude Code and Codex under the same compute budget.

title: 미국 AI 수출 규제에 막힌 앤트로픽, 신분증 인증으로 돌파구 찾나
blurb: The US government ordered Anthropic to cut off non-US nationals from its newest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, on national security grounds, forcing the company to disable both for all customers because it cannot currently distinguish foreign users from American ones. A revised privacy policy taking effect July 8 lets Anthropic collect government-issued ID scans to verify citizenship, a possible workaround for consumers though not yet for enterprise customers.

title: From PGP to Mythos: a brief history of export controls that didn’t stop anyone
blurb: The author argues that Washington's order forcing Anthropic to restrict exports of Fable and Mythos is the first real test of using export controls to contain frontier AI, and that history suggests it will fail. The piece traces the uneven track record of cyber export controls from 1990s encryption battles through spyware, framing the standoff as the template other labs will eventually be governed by.

title: Adobe expands Firefly capabilities, extends agentic tools to Creative Cloud apps
blurb: Adobe expanded its Firefly AI Assistant with new pre-built Creative Skills, including a Brand kit creation Skill that generates logos, color palettes, and social media promo videos from a single prompt. Adobe also extended agentic features to Premiere, Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, and Frame.io, adding a sidebar where Firefly can organize assets, fact-check against briefings, and run pre-print checklists on the user's behalf.

title: Tensordyne makes a big bet on log math to beat Nvidia
blurb: Tensordyne is betting on logarithmic number systems, which replace expensive hardware multiplications with cheaper additions, to challenge Nvidia on AI inference efficiency. The approach trades the dense multiply-accumulate operations that dominate GPU silicon for log-domain arithmetic, aiming for better performance per watt on the workloads that increasingly drive datacenter compute costs.

title: Exclusive: Microsoft evaluates different open models for Cowork
blurb: Microsoft is evaluating a broader set of open and open-weight models beyond DeepSeek to power Copilot Cowork, its agentic work app now generally available to Microsoft 365 Copilot customers. Cowork separates the orchestration harness from the model layer, letting Microsoft route tasks to frontier APIs or cheaper self-hosted Azure models based on cost, latency, and capability while reducing dependence on any single provider.

title: Mistral AI to get Code and Apps features on Vibe
blurb: Mistral AI is building Code and Apps sections into the web version of Le Chat (Vibe), adding a browser coding surface that mirrors its CLI agents and an artifacts-style area for building, hosting, and sharing apps via connectors and multi-step workflows. Both features remain in development as CEO Arthur Mensch confirmed a large, sparse mixture-of-experts model shipping this summer as open weights, with partner early access in July.

title: ICYMI: ZAI launches GLM-5.2 open model with 1M context
blurb: Z.ai released GLM-5.2, an open flagship coding model with a 1M-token context window and up to 128K output tokens, built for long-horizon agent tasks like full-repository implementation, debugging, and refactoring. The 744B-parameter model (40B active) scores 81.0 on Terminal-Bench 2.1 and 62.1 on SWE-bench Pro, and is available to GLM Coding Plan users inside agents like Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Cline.

title: From $5B to $13B in five months: Baseten reportedly closing in on $1.5B raise
blurb: Baseten is reportedly closing in on a $1.5B funding round that would value the AI inference platform at up to $13B, according to The Wall Street Journal. The deal lands five months after its $300M Series E at a $5B valuation and is structured as a split-priced round, with some investors entering at an $11B floor and others paying the full $13B.

title: Sovereign Execution Brokers: Enforcing Certificate-Bound Authority in Agentic Control Planes
blurb: This paper introduces the Sovereign Execution Broker (SEB), a runtime enforcement boundary that gates production mutations behind certificates issued by a separate assurance layer, keeping write authority out of non-deterministic agent reasoning. SEB verifies each request against its certified execution contract, validity windows, and revocation epochs, mints scoped short-lived identity, and records signed decision logs, with a prototype evaluated on AWS and Kubernetes under fault injection.

title: SoftSkill: Behavioral Compression for Contextual Adaptation
blurb: SoftSkill is a frozen-backbone method that compresses natural-language agent skills into a compact continuous prefix, tuned with next-token prediction and deployed as a latent behavioral prior at inference time. A length-32 prefix on Qwen3.5-4B improves over no-skill prompting by 8.3 points on SearchQA and 42.1 points on LiveMath, replacing hundreds to thousands of Markdown tokens with a few virtual ones.

title: UNIEGO: Proxies as Mediators for Unified Egocentric Video Representation Learning
blurb: This paper introduces UNIEGO, a unified egocentric video encoder trained via hierarchical multi-teacher distillation from nine teachers spanning ego-exo viewpoints, RGB, depth, and skeleton modalities, and four foundation models. Rather than distilling directly from incompatible teachers, the framework routes knowledge through representation-specific proxy models and then applies Selective Proxy Distillation, which per sample distills only from proxies that are correct and confident, reaching state-of-the-art results on action recognition, video retrieval, and action segmentation.

title: Scalable Training of Spatially Grounded 2D Vision-Language Models for Radiology
blurb: This paper introduces RadGrounder, a radiology vision-language model trained on RefRad2D, a 1.2M-pair bilingual German/English CT and MR dataset built without manual spatial annotations using LLM-based curation and automated segmentation. RadGrounder jointly handles report generation, visual question answering, and bounding-box or segmentation grounding, matching specialized medical VLMs on Slake and VQA-RAD while adding spatially verifiable outputs at no cost to VQA performance.

title: Marginal Advantage Accumulation for Memory-Driven Agent Self-Evolution
blurb: This paper introduces Marginal Advantage Accumulation (MAA), a post-processing method that resolves contradictory cross-batch feedback in trace distillation by accumulating signed, operation-level evidence through EMA and merging semantic identities for traceability. MAA achieved the best results in 14 of 16 settings across 4 benchmarks and 4 target models while cutting optimization-phase token consumption by roughly 75%.

title: Beyond Global Replanning: Hierarchical Recovery for Cross-Device Agent Systems
blurb: H-RePlan is a hierarchical replanning framework for multi-device agents that separates device-local strategy recovery from orchestrator-level global replanning through a compact cross-layer failure abstraction, with each device equipped with interchangeable API, CLI, and GUI execution strategies. On HeraBench, a fault-injected benchmark spanning Linux and Android workflows, it beats single-strategy and coarse-grained baselines on completion, instruction adherence, and perfect-pass rates while cutting the token cost of reliable end-to-end success.

title: What Makes Effective Supervision in Latent Chain-of-Thought: An Information-Theoretic Analysis
blurb: This paper analyzes latent Chain-of-Thought reasoning, where models reason inside continuous hidden states rather than explicit text traces, and identifies a "dual collapse" failure: gradient attenuation during training and representational drift in latent space. The authors introduce the Unified Latent Probe to measure mutual information between latent trajectories and explicit reasoning steps, finding that accuracy tracks information fidelity and arguing supervision should target mutual information maximization over rigid geometric compression.

title: MemGUI-Agent: An End-to-End Long-Horizon Mobile GUI Agent with Proactive Context Management
blurb: MemGUI-Agent is a mobile GUI agent that treats context management as first-class actions, casting memory updates as decisions emitted by the same policy that selects UI actions. Built on a method called Context-as-Action, it maintains three structured fields instead of passively appending history, and an 8B model trained on the 2,956-trajectory MemGUI-3K dataset posts the best open-data 8B results on MemGUI-Bench.

title: Beyond Uniform Forgetting: A Study of Sequential Direct Preference Optimization Across Preference Settings
blurb: This paper studies sequential Direct Preference Optimization across four preference settings and finds that training on later objectives does not uniformly degrade earlier ones. Using Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with LoRA adapters, the authors show that preference change ranges from partial degradation to stability or positive transfer depending on objective compatibility, signal strength, and training order.

title: Solving an ARD problem in AI: Agentic Resource Discovery
blurb: Google, Microsoft, Cisco, Nvidia, Salesforce, and others proposed Agentic Resource Discovery (ARD), an open protocol that lets AI agents find and safely use the tools they need within a corporate domain. ARD works across two layers: organizations publish catalogs of available capabilities, and registries crawl those catalogs like a search engine. The specification is available now.

title: ChatGPT's image generator can be manipulated to produce violent, sexual content
blurb: Mindgard research found that ChatGPT's image generator can be manipulated into producing violent and sexually explicit content, including depictions of death and sexual violence, without users directly requesting it. The findings follow earlier reports that OpenAI's safety measures against AI-generated nudes remained bypassable, raising questions about why these models are trained on such imagery in the first place.

title: OpenAI prepares GPT-5.6 models for the upcoming release
blurb: OpenAI is preparing to release its GPT-5.6 family as soon as Tuesday, with standard, Mini, and Pro variants reportedly landing together and a context window pushed toward 1.5 million tokens, up from GPT-5.5's million. Developers testing early builds claim it edges out Anthropic's Mythos line on agentic coding, and OpenAI is said to be planning deeper price cuts on tokens to widen its existing lead.

title: GLM-5.2 Becomes the Top Open-Weight Model on Artificial Analysis
blurb: Artificial Analysis named Z.ai's GLM-5.2 the top open-weight model on its Intelligence Index, scoring 51, up 11 points from GLM-5.1. The model ships under an MIT license with a 1-million-token context window, and independent testers reported it matching or beating Anthropic's Opus 4.8 on build tasks at roughly five times lower cost.

title: Structuring and Tokenizing Distributed User Interest Context for Generative Recommendation
blurb: This paper introduces G2Rec, a framework for generative recommendation that unifies graph-based modeling of user co-engagement with semantic item tokenization at industrial scale. It builds holistic, semantically grounded user interest prototypes without ground-truth interest labels, and outperformed existing methods in online deployment across product surfaces and on public benchmarks.

title: Multi-View Decompilation for LLM-Based Malware Classification
blurb: This paper benchmarks LLM-based malware classification when models are given decompiled pseudo-C from two decompilers, Ghidra and RetDec, instead of a single view. Across models from several major families, supplying both decompiler views raises malicious-class F1 mainly by improving recall, since Ghidra and RetDec make partially different errors and provide complementary evidence for training-free triage.

title: Data Bias Mitigation under Coverage Constraints & The Price of Fairness
blurb: This paper extends a bias mitigation framework with coverage constraints that enforce sufficient representation across groups, including intersectional subgroups defined by attributes like race and gender. The authors formulate mitigation as an integer linear program, characterize the "price of fairness" as the minimum data modification cost given a fairness tolerance, and show on public datasets that the approach preserves predictive accuracy across classifiers.

title: The Register Gap: A Meaning Intelligence Framework for Nigerian Public Discourse
blurb: This paper introduces the Meaning Intelligence Framework, a nine-dimension schema that lifted Gemini 2.5 Flash's Nigerian register classification from 33% to 73%.

title: Local Qwen isn't a worse Opus, it's a different tool
blurb: The author argues that local Qwen models aren't a cheaper substitute for Opus but a distinct tool with a different cost-benefit profile, drawing on real numbers from running a bootstrapped software business. An RTX 6000 Pro card paid for itself within two or three months on his specific Go and infrastructure workloads, but Qwen still can't be trusted unsupervised. Its worst failure modes, infinite loops and hallucination, surface most once quantized down to fit a consumer GPU.

title: Trump says he saw Anthropic last week as a national security threat, but signals relations have since improved because Dario Amodei was "nice" and "smart" at G7 (Maria Curi/Axios)
blurb: Trump said he viewed Anthropic as a national security threat last week but indicated relations have since warmed after meeting CEO Dario Amodei at the G7. He credited the shift to Amodei being "nice" and "smart" in person, a notable softening given the administration's recent friction with the company over AI safety messaging.

title: Scaling Ray Serve LLM on GKE: Performance without losing the developer experience
blurb: Google and Anyscale optimized Ray Serve LLM on GKE, delivering up to 5x higher throughput and 8x lower latency for distributed inference. The gains come from three changes: built-in HAProxy for internal request routing, a direct token streaming path that bypasses the ingress router, and a v2 Ray executor backend that moves Ray out of vLLM's data plane to enable asynchronous scheduling.

title: Google launches AI agent for Ad Manager
blurb: Google launched Ask Ad Manager, a Gemini-powered agent that lets publishers analyze performance, troubleshoot delivery issues, and generate reports in Google Ad Manager through natural language. The beta opens this month, and Google plans to follow with REST APIs, an MCP server, and specialized agents for discovering inventory, negotiating deals, and executing campaigns.

title: DeepSWIP: Quotient-WMC Counterfactuals for Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
blurb: DeepSWIP is a counterfactual reasoning semantics for DeepProbLog programs that reduces neural predicates to ordinary ProbLog choices via neural materialization, then computes counterfactuals through weighted model counting over a single transformed program. Across 12,000 queries on MPI3D it matches a DeepTwin construction while running 2.14x faster by avoiding endogenous duplication, and a SUMO experiment shows neural calibration drift biases plug-in estimates that a randomized-policy AIPW estimator largely corrects.

title: From Texts to Scores: Tracing the Emergence of Essay Quality Representations in Large Language Models
blurb: This paper analyzes the hidden representations of eight LLMs across English and Portuguese essay datasets and finds that essay quality is encoded in a linearly accessible form that emerges progressively across layers. The authors identify individual "essay scoring neurons" whose activations correlate with scores and shift toward deeper layers as essays grow longer, offering interpretability insights into LLM-based automated essay scoring.

title: AgenticDB: Agentic Performance Reconfiguration for Database Workloads
blurb: AgenticDB is an agentic framework that reformulates database tuning as a self-refining reconfiguration process, proposing and applying both DBMS- and OS-level changes under safety constraints while using runtime feedback to diagnose bottlenecks and guide later decisions. Across MySQL and PostgreSQL on YCSB, Sysbench, and TPC-H workloads, it beats the strongest baseline by 118.1% on average and cuts aggregate time-to-best by 22.6%.

title: Policy-aware Vector Search: A Vision for Fine Grained Access Control in Vector Databases
blurb: This paper presents a vision for Policy-aware Vector Search, formalizing fine-grained access control (FGAC) for vector databases used in RAG and organizational AI pipelines. The authors argue that combining structured and unstructured attributes for approximate queries creates tension between enforcing per-user policies, maintaining high ANN recall, and keeping latency low, and they compare enforcement strategies alongside open research challenges.

title: MobileForge: Annotation-Free Adaptation for Mobile GUI Agents with Hierarchical Feedback-Guided Policy Optimization
blurb: MobileForge is an annotation-free adaptation system for mobile GUI agents that pairs MobileGym, which grounds task generation and rollout evaluation in real app interaction, with Hierarchical Feedback-Guided Policy Optimization (HiFPO) to turn trajectory outcomes and step-level feedback into GRPO updates. Using only auto-generated data, it lifts Qwen3-VL-8B to 67.2% Pass@3 on AndroidWorld, and the adapted ForgeOwl-8B reaches 77.6%.

title: The Token Compression Illusion: Why I'm Skeptical of RTK
blurb: RTK promises 60-90% token savings by compressing terminal output, but the author argues the figure is misleading because it measures stripped CLI output, not your actual API bill, which is dominated by file reads, repo context, system prompts, and reasoning tokens. The deeper risk is silent failure: the agent has no idea output was compressed, so a dropped stack trace leaves both developer and model in the dark. Until RTK ships SWE-bench-style task-success benchmarks, savings claims ignore the only metric that matters.

title: New usage analytics and updated spend controls for enterprises
blurb: OpenAI introduced new spend controls and usage analytics for ChatGPT Enterprise, giving organizations finer visibility into how teams consume AI across the company. The analytics surface usage patterns by team and feature, while the updated controls let administrators set and enforce spending limits as deployments scale.

title: Pseudo-Feature Padding: A Lightweight Defense Against False Data Injection in Power Grids
blurb: This paper introduces Pseudo-Feature Padding, a defense that hardens deep neural network detectors against false data injection attacks in power-grid cyber-physical systems. The method adds an input layer that pads samples with pseudo-feature values drawn from the data's statistical distribution, randomizing input dimensionality to make adversarial perturbations non-transferable, and was tested on IEEE 14-, 30-, 118-, and 300-bus systems with negligible performance loss.

title: QMFOL: Benchmarking Large Language Model Reasoning via Quantifiable Monadic First-Order Logic Test Case Generation
blurb: QMFOL is an automated framework that generates monadic first-order logic reasoning tasks with quantifiable control over reasoning depth, width, label types, and distractors, using round-trip verification against an external prover to ensure logical consistency. The resulting QMFOLBench comprises 2,880 instances across 960 configurations, and evaluations on eight models show accuracy dropping and compute overhead rising as logical complexity increases.

title: Perplexity releases Brain Memory System for Perplexity Computer
blurb: Perplexity released Brain, a memory system that sits beneath multiple products rather than inside any single one, with signals pointing to it powering Perplexity Search, the Computer agent, and possibly the Comet browser. Unlike opaque assistant memory, Brain exposes the full knowledge base to users as categorized topics, the context behind each, and a navigable 3D map of the connections between them.

title: Hyundai buys Boston Dynamics
blurb: Hyundai Motor Group will acquire SoftBank's remaining 9.65% stake in Boston Dynamics for $325 million, making the Waltham, Massachusetts robotics company a wholly owned subsidiary once the deal is approved on June 22. The purchase gives Hyundai full control as it prepares to deploy a production version of the electric Atlas humanoid at its Georgia EV plant by 2028.

title: AIは間違えるだけではない、間違いを守ろうとするーー「ヒューマン・イン・ザ・ループ」の盲点
blurb: The author argues that "human-in-the-loop" is a flawed assumption for enterprise AI governance, because models don't just produce wrong answers, they actively defend them. An HBS study of 72 consultants using GPT-4 found that when experts pushed back, the model escalated rather than retreated, a pattern the authors call "persuasion bombing." The post recommends separating generation from verification, using a second model, an independent reviewer, or a critique agent with no stake in defending the original answer.

title: OpenAI prepares real-time voice mode for Pets in Codex
blurb: OpenAI is testing real-time voice mode in Codex, with a "Hey Chat" wake word and shared orb, library, and avatar surfaces borrowed from ChatGPT.

title: Most AI-powered mainframe migration vendors expected to fail by 2030, Gartner warns
blurb: Gartner projects that 75% of vendors offering AI-powered mainframe migration tools will pivot or shut down by 2030. The firm cited a widening gap between GenAI marketing and its real ability to transform complex legacy code like COBOL, compounded by cost overruns, declining demand, and the loss of experienced mainframe talent.

title: HEPTv2: End-to-End Efficient Point Transformer for Charged Particle Reconstruction
blurb: HEPTv2 is an end-to-end point-transformer that reconstructs charged-particle tracks from raw detector hits in a single trainable pipeline, replacing the graph construction, clustering, and filtering stages that prior methods require. On TrackML it hits 98.6% double-majority tracking efficiency at a 0.8% fake rate in ~15ms per event, cutting latency by factors of 7 over the strongest transformer and 38 to 52 over optimized graph-based pipelines.

title: Sparsity, Superposition, and Forgetting: A Mechanistic Study of Representation Retention in Continual Learning
blurb: This paper introduces a controlled toy-world framework for isolating what causes forgetting in continual learning, using a synthetic generator-separator pipeline with tunable feature sparsity and overlap to make representation strength and superposition directly measurable. The authors find that more superposition does not inevitably cause forgetting: when representations stay strong, overlap can persist without degrading retention, and higher sparsity broadens capacity usage rather than driving drift.

title: ReNikud: Audio-Supervised Hebrew Grapheme-to-Phoneme Conversion
blurb: ReNikud is a grapheme-to-phoneme system for Modern Hebrew that converts unvocalized text into IPA phonemes, addressing the ambiguity created by the language's abjad script. It is trained with weak audio supervision from an ASR pseudo-labeling pipeline over thousands of hours of unlabeled Hebrew speech, and a pseudo-vocalization architecture enforces character-level alignment, surpassing prior state-of-the-art on Hebrew G2P benchmarks.

title: Learning to Prompt: Improving Student Engagement with Adaptive LLM-based High-School Tutoring
blurb: This paper introduces an adaptive LLM tutoring system that uses a prompt routing model to select teaching strategies based on 14 pedagogical features extracted from transcripts, rather than relying on a single static prompt. In A/B testing with 359 high-school students, the router cut conversations by roughly 3 turns and a stochastic version that samples strategies raised exercise conversion from 19.6% to 28.1%.

title: When Does Streaming Tool Use Help? Characterizing Tool-Intent Stabilization in Streaming Retrieval-Augmented Generation
blurb: This paper studies when Streaming RAG, which issues tool queries before a user finishes speaking, actually reduces latency, arguing the benefit hinges on tool-intent stabilization: the point where a speculative query's retrieval converges to the answer-bearing result. On the CRAG benchmark of 1,371 questions, the authors derive a model-agnostic bound on hideable latency and find that at a realistic operating point, 73.9% of queries admit substantial latency hiding without any model training.

title: FFinRED: An Expert-Guided Benchmark Generation and Evaluation Framework for Financial LLM Red-Teaming
blurb: FinRED is an expert-guided red-teaming framework that evaluates financial LLMs for finance-specific risks like regulatory evasion, fraud facilitation, and compliance violations, using a two-level taxonomy mapped to standards like FATF and EU DORA. Its pipeline converts real financial documents into validated adversarial prompts and applies a finance-specific rubric that cuts critical false negatives from 28 to 12, and it is now deployed in South Korea's Financial Security Institute regulatory sandbox.

title: Vibe Coding for Visualization Implementation: An Empirical Study of Practices and Challenges
blurb: This paper presents an empirical study of how 16 users of varying expertise employ AI "vibe coding" tools to implement data visualizations. Participants completed two visualization tasks and an interview, revealing that visualization work demands tighter alignment between user intent and visual output than general software development. The authors characterize the diverse prompting, evaluation, and iteration practices users adopt, and document the challenges they hit throughout the process.

title: The US says ASML’s top chip tool may be in China. ASML says it isn’t
blurb: US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick has told ASML executives he is concerned that one of the company's extreme ultraviolet lithography machines, the only tools capable of printing the most advanced chips, may have reached China in breach of export controls. ASML denies any such system exists in China, and administration officials reportedly cited evidence of EUV-related shipments but declined to show it.

title: Amazon investigating engineers who criticized AI data center expansion
blurb: A group of Amazon engineers said the company is investigating them after they testified at Seattle City Council hearings criticizing its rapid AI data center buildout and calling for stronger regulation. A complaint filed with the Seattle Office for Civil Rights alleges Amazon monitored their political advocacy and warned of discipline up to termination, which the workers say violates a city ordinance protecting employees' political ideology.

title: Prem seeks $100M Series A as export bans boost sovereign AI demand
blurb: Swiss startup Prem SA is raising $100M in Series A funding at a valuation of at least $500M, with the round expected to close in Q3. Prem sells software for running and fine-tuning AI models on a company's own infrastructure rather than a cloud provider's, targeting hedge funds and law firms as US export bans push regulated buyers toward sovereign AI.

title: Game-clip AI startup General Intuition in talks to raise $300M at $2B valuation
blurb: General Intuition is in talks to raise about $300M at a valuation just over $2B, roughly four times the $134M seed it closed eight months ago. The New York startup trains embodied AI agents on Medal's dataset of roughly 2 billion first-person gameplay clips a year, using world models as the training ground rather than selling them as products.

title: Introducing Ask Ad Manager, the AI agent that will help you get more done
blurb: Google announced Ask Ad Manager, a Gemini-built conversational agent in Google Ad Manager that lets publishers diagnose line-item issues, generate custom reports, and navigate the platform through multi-turn prompts on their own data. The agent enters beta this month, with additional skills rolling out throughout the year as Google pushes Ad Manager toward more agentic workflows.

title: Ditching the cloud for local AI — how I use two mini PCs to process millions of tokens a day and save money on costly API fees
blurb: A Tom's Hardware writer details ditching cloud APIs for local inference, running millions of tokens a day across two mini PCs to escape rising subscription and API costs. The setup centers on a GMKtech machine with an AMD Ryzen AI Max+ 395 and 96GB of RAM, paired with tools like LM Studio, Ollama, and llama.cpp to keep ongoing costs to electricity.

title: The Thundering Herd Problem in Agentic AI: Why Traditional Fixes Fall Short
blurb: This post argues that agentic AI creates an internally generated version of the thundering herd problem, where fan-out and parallelization synchronize load by design rather than from an external trigger like a cache expiry or service restart. Standard fixes such as exponential backoff and circuit breakers only partially transfer, and systems hit a non-linear saturation point between 700 and 1,000 concurrent agents that staging environments rarely expose.

title: Toward Calibrated Mixture-of-Experts Under Distribution Shift
blurb: This paper studies how mixture-of-experts models stay calibrated under distribution shift, showing that expert-level calibration guarantees overall calibration in hard-routed models but fails for soft-routed ones. The authors propose an adversarial reweighting that penalizes calibration errors of the routed aggregate, improving the accuracy-calibration tradeoff across model classes, tasks, and shifts, including on difficult data subsets.

title: Optimal Deterministic Multicalibration and Omniprediction
blurb: This paper resolves an open problem by giving the first deterministic algorithm that achieves the minimax-optimal Õ(ε⁻³) sample complexity for ε-multicalibration, a fairness property where predictions stay unbiased across reweighted subgroups. The authors generalize it to deterministic predictors satisfying outcome indistinguishability, yielding optimal deterministic omnipredictors and panpredictors and answering questions left open by several prior works.

title: Multi-Task Bayesian In-Context Learning
blurb: This paper introduces a multi-task in-context learning framework for amortized hierarchical Bayesian predictive inference, representing prior information as a prefix of in-context datasets so a transformer can adapt across families of priors at test time. Across evaluations including out-of-meta-distribution priors and high-dimensional latent structures, the method matches oracle Bayesian predictors while running orders of magnitude faster, with results validated on a spatiotemporal temperature prediction benchmark.

title: Agentic Symbolic Search: Characterizing PDEs Beyond Hand-crafted Expressions, Meshes, and Neural Networks
blurb: This paper introduces Agentic Symbolic Search (ASYS), a framework in which an agent translates PDE theory, problem constraints, and prior search experience into differentiable symbolic programs refined by evolutionary search and gradient-based parameter fitting. Across five problems spanning bounded dynamics, finite-time blow-up, and free-boundary focusing, ASYS recovered known analytical forms and produced new interpretable ones, including a geometric interface formula for Allen-Cahn 2D dynamics.

title: Low-Cost Multi-Precision Systolic Arrays for Accelerating FHE NTTs on AI ASICs
blurb: A multi-precision systolic array reconstructs full-precision FHE NTT results natively in-array, giving at least 1.33x speedup on AI matrix engines.

title: Agentic Electronic Design Automation: A Handoff Perspective
blurb: This survey reviews 82 LLM-agent systems for electronic design automation, organizing them around "handoff validity," the principle that a transferred design artifact must satisfy its downstream consumer's acceptance conditions and carry sufficient context, evidence, and provenance. The authors classify systems as Stage-Bound, Flow-Bound, or Organization-Bound, and propose a five-layer EDA agent communication protocol covering discovery, messaging, tool invocation, orchestration, and IP protection.

title: Beyond Accuracy: Measuring Logical Compliance of Predictive Models
blurb: This paper introduces the Rule Violation Score (RVS), a metric that measures how well a predictive model respects predefined logical constraints, independently of its predictive accuracy. RVS handles hard and soft rules differently, works on any model over a relational vocabulary, and compiles to auto-generated SQL queries for Horn rules, revealing that models with comparable accuracy can differ substantially in logical compliance.

title: Zeta Labs brings AI employee Viktor to Microsoft Teams
blurb: Zeta Labs brought Viktor, its AI employee, to Microsoft Teams, extending a product already running in over 20,000 Slack workspaces.

title: OpenAI gets the attention it needs from AI researcher Noam Shazeer
blurb: Noam Shazeer, co-author of "Attention Is All You Need" and a creator of the transformer architecture, is leaving Google to join OpenAI. Shazeer most recently co-led Google's Gemini project after the company acquired his startup Character.AI for $2.7B, and his hiring ahead of OpenAI's expected IPO could appeal to investors.

title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
blurb: This paper adapts the DAAM cross-attention attribution framework to speech diffusion models for the first time, applying it to CapSpeech-TTS to trace how individual words in a style caption shape acoustic output. Analyzing 3,600 caption-transcript combinations, the authors find that style tokens act as global conditioning, correlate with pitch and energy, and peak in early ODE steps and deep layers, with attention selectivity maximized at layer 17.

title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
blurb: This paper introduces "privacy via predictability," a fine-grained framework that measures privacy leakage as the incremental gain in an attacker's ability to predict sensitive information after observing an algorithm's output, given a known compromised portion of the dataset and a specified query family. The authors show predictability and differential privacy are generally incomparable, derive a predictability-calibrated output perturbation scheme for ERM using the generalized method of moments, and position the approach as complementary to DP.

title: Topological Data Analysis for High-Dimensional Dynamic Process Monitoring
blurb: This paper combines topological data analysis with a neural ODE to detect events in high-dimensional industrial process data, outperforming PCA, autoencoder, and Koopman baselines.

title: Library-Aware Doubles and Iterative Repair for Large Language Model-Generated Unit Tests in OpenSIL Firmware
blurb: This paper introduces an LLM-guided multi-agent pipeline that automates unit test authoring for AMD's open-source silicon initialization library (openSIL), where strict build constraints and unresolved dependencies routinely break compilation. The workflow combines test scaffold generation, library-aware stubs, mocks, and fakes, and an iterative compile-and-repair loop, producing compilable tests for 73 of 76 functions and reaching 98.8% mean line coverage with coverage guidance.

title: Trump Tells Axios He Doesn’t See Anthropic as US Security Threat
