title: Flipkart Wins CNCF End User Case Study Contest for Kubernetes and Chaos Engineering Scale
blurb: Flipkart won the CNCF End User Case Study Contest for KubeCon + CloudNativeCon India 2026 for its central reliability engineering team's multi-tenant chaos engineering platform, built on Kubernetes and the CNCF incubating project LitmusChaos to run roughly 90% of fault-injection experiments in staging before high-traffic festive sales. The team shipped four custom LitmusChaos extensions, including a DaemonSet-based high-availability model for parallel injection, and contributed five fixes upstream.

title: Companies are discarding the logs they need to catch a breach
blurb: A Dynatrace survey of 450 senior IT leaders found that half of large enterprises drop or never collect an average of 86% of their logs, deleting them on purpose to control costs that average $2.5M a year and eat roughly half of observability budgets. The trade-off lands on security teams, who reach for those same logs during forensics and incident response, only to find the evidence sampled away or aged out before a breach surfaces.

title: Amazon ECS introduces new high-resolution metrics for faster service auto scaling
blurb: Amazon ECS service auto scaling now supports high-resolution 20-second metrics, cutting the time to trigger scale-out from 363 to 86 seconds and total scale-and-provision time from 386 to 109 seconds in AWS benchmarks. Faster reaction lets teams trim baseline task counts and the preemptive capacity padding that hedges against traffic spikes, lowering compute cost while target tracking replaces the custom step-scaling policies that used to deliver aggressive scaling.

title: Many Let's Encrypt renewals had errors today
blurb: Let's Encrypt's ACME v02 production API returned errors on a portion of certificate renewals today before the service recovered to a fully operational state across both High Assurance datacenters. Teams running short-lived certs with tight auto-renewal windows should confirm their cert-manager or Certbot retries succeeded, since a failed renewal that slips past expiry triggers TLS outages rather than a graceful retry.

title: AI Catalog updates for governance and operations
blurb: GitLab 19.1 adds event-driven triggers to Duo Flows in the AI Catalog, letting flows fire on pipeline failure, success, or cancellation, on merge conflicts, and on draft-to-ready transitions without a human clicking through the UI. The release pairs the triggers with governance controls and config validation so admins can audit what automation runs in their environment and who deployed it.

title: Scaling Ray Serve LLM on GKE: Performance without losing the developer experience
blurb: Google and Anyscale rebuilt Ray Serve LLM on GKE for up to 5x higher throughput and 8x lower latency, while keeping its Python-native serving APIs intact. Three changes drive it: a built-in HAProxy layer that absorbs request routing so the Python runtime stops saturating under load, direct token streaming that bypasses the ingress router on the return path, and a v2 vLLM executor that moves Ray out of the data plane for async scheduling. Benchmarks ran Gemma 4 E2B on A4 VMs with NVIDIA HGX B200.

title: Kubernetes-based virtualization proves its enterprise credentials on critical rail infrastructure
blurb: CSX Corp., one of the largest US freight railroads, standardized its remaining on-prem workloads on Red Hat OpenShift Virtualization with Portworx storage, running safety-critical systems like Positive Train Control on the same Kubernetes platform as its containers. The move followed a head-to-head proof of concept scored on resiliency, live-migration speed, and operational overhead, and reflects a push to avoid lock-in after recent consolidation in the legacy virtualization market.

title: One vulnerability view: From scanner coverage to AI governance
blurb: GitLab 19.1 adds a single vulnerability view that enforces any SARIF-emitting third-party scanner across every project under one policy, eliminating the per-project drift that lets new repos ship unscanned for weeks. Third-party findings now flow through GitLab Duo's auto-remediation, with SAST false-positive triage and agentic resolution opening ready-to-merge fixes, plus a beta of AI audit event streaming to track agent behavior.

title: Surprising economics of load-balanced systems
blurb: Marc Brooker works through an M/M/c queuing model where c single-request servers sit behind a load balancer, with offered load scaled linearly to hold per-server utilization constant. The counterintuitive result, derived from Erlang's C formula, is that mean client latency drops toward the one-second service floor as c grows, because larger pools queue a shrinking fraction of requests. The practical takeaway for capacity planning: bigger shared pools beat many small ones at the same utilization.

title: Adobe embeds agentic AI workflows across Creative Cloud, shifting from media generation to production orchestration
blurb: Adobe released a Creative Agent in public beta across Premiere Pro, Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, and Frame.io, shifting its AI from flat media generation to orchestrating multi-step production work via each app's native APIs. For marketing teams, the pitch is delegating repetitive tasks like batch-renaming clips and updating brand assets across layouts, while a new Firefly "Elements" library keeps characters and objects visually consistent as campaigns scale.

title: Generative Engine Optimization at Scale: Measuring Brand Visibility Across AI Search Engines
blurb: A new analysis of 100K+ AI-search responses across 100+ brands quantifies how visibility splits by brand maturity: global names like Stripe and Nike appear in 73% of relevant answers, mid-market brands in 44%, and niche or early-stage brands in just 11%. Citations concentrate where marketers can act, with 78% pointing to corporate sites and ranked "best-of" listicles the single most-cited format at 21%. Sentiment is the volatile signal, flipping 6.7x more often than whether a brand gets mentioned at all.

title: Google AI Overviews cite self-serving listicles, but recommend competitors 69% of the time
blurb: Google AI Overviews cited brands' own self-promotional "best" listicles while recommending competitors instead 69% of the time, according to Lily Ray's analysis of 100 B2B software queries. Sites that leaned on self-ranking listicles saw organic traffic decline starting in January and accelerate through the May 2026 core update, as Google shifted citations toward Reddit, Forbes, and YouTube.

title: Pew: 60% of Americans read AI summaries in search results
blurb: A new Pew Research report found that 60% of U.S. adults now read AI-generated summaries at the top of search results, and 40% use chatbots like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Copilot to look up information, the most common chatbot activity Pew measured. With ChatGPT reaching 44% of adults and daily chatbot use at one in four, traditional search rankings no longer capture every surface where people find answers.

title: How We Rebuilt AI Visibility Measurement From The Ground Up
blurb: NP Digital rebuilt its AI visibility audits around the SPIV framework (Segment, Persona, Intent, Variable), which grounds tracking prompts in real user data instead of generic inputs that produce unactionable results. Because model responses are probabilistic, visibility is expressed as a distribution, with a second layer of calculated metrics like Shannon entropy, Gini coefficient, and KL divergence exposing the stability and competitive dynamics behind the surface numbers. The payoff is a forcing function: it gets harder to justify tracking low-intent queries that have no connection to how the product is actually bought.

title: The May Core Update Made Reddit Google’s Default Answer
blurb: Reddit's share of Google's top-3 results rose to 10.24% after the May 2026 core update, up from 8.56% in March, with the number of keywords where Reddit holds the #1 spot jumping 54% to 13,872. The gains skewed toward experience-led queries, but B2B is no longer exempt: Technology climbed from 4.77% to 7.03%, Business hit 12.17%, and Career and Jobs sits near 17%.

title: 10 SEO Trends I’ve Seen Firsthand in 2026 (With Data)
blurb: Searches for "query fan-out" are up 2,550% year over year, reflecting how AI search engines now split a single prompt into a dozen hidden sub-queries and cite the pages that answer them. Since chasing specific fan-outs is futile (they change with every generation), the Ahrefs data points to building topic clusters with full topical coverage instead. The same logic now shapes Google ranking, where AI Overviews increasingly keep users on the results page rather than sending the click.

title: How NerdWallet Built a High-EEAT Review Program That Buyers and LLMs Trust
blurb: NerdWallet's review pages capture AI visibility that brands cannot: in FinTech, only 11.4% of sources cited in AI answers are brand-owned, with NerdWallet and Bankrate sitting near the top of the rest. The site's nearly 9,000 pages pull over 7 million organic visits a month worth an estimated $24 million, built on a decade of EEAT signals Google and LLMs both reward. The B2B takeaway is to publish independent, expert-vetted comparison content that ranks products against each other rather than pitching your own.

title: Agentic marketing AI startup Gradial grabs $65M in fresh funding
blurb: Gradial raised $65M in a Series C led by Insight Partners at a $675M valuation, bringing total funding to $110M over the past 16 months. Rather than building another point tool, the Seattle startup runs an orchestration layer of AI agents across Salesforce, Adobe, ServiceNow, and Databricks to author, brand-check, and publish marketing content through existing approval flows.

title: OpenAI opens ChatGPT Ads Manager beta to UK advertisers
blurb: OpenAI began rolling out a self-serve ChatGPT Ads Manager beta to UK advertisers, with a four-area dashboard for campaigns, tools, billing, and settings, and no upfront billing required to create an account. The beta lacks Google Ads-style multi-account management, so agencies must be invited into client accounts individually, but it gives UK brands an early read on a new ad channel before targeting and measurement tools arrive.

title: How Attention.com Turns Sales Calls Into Pipeline: The Best GTM Data You Own, and Why Most B2B Teams Throw It Away
blurb: At SaaStr AI 2026, Attention.com CEO Anis Bennaceur argued that next quarter's pipeline already sits in the sales calls you recorded this quarter and never mined. His core play: rebuild your ICP monthly off live closed-won data instead of annually, using an agent that pulls recent wins, extracts buyer firmographics and tenure from call transcripts, and enriches them in a tool like Clay. The output is a dozen narrow buyer pockets you can write real emails to, like "the frustrated veteran stuck on an incumbent," rather than one stale broad ICP.

title: Introducing Ask Ad Manager, the AI agent that will help you get more done
blurb: Google introduced Ask Ad Manager, a Gemini-built conversational agent that lets publishers troubleshoot line items, generate custom reports, and navigate the platform through prompts using only their own data. The beta ships this month, signaling Google's push to make ad-tech workflows agentic and pull publishers from manual report-stitching toward faster diagnosis and revenue recovery.

title: Google Ads automatically enrols advertisers in conversion-based customer lists
blurb: Google Ads will automatically enable conversion-based customer lists on Aug. 18 for eligible advertisers already running Enhanced Conversions and Customer Match, building first-party audiences from existing conversion data with no setup required. Advertisers who don't want the lists generated must opt out in account settings before that date, since Google will start processing the data and creating the segments automatically afterward.

title: Google Ads launches beta for supplemental conversion data
blurb: Google Ads is rolling out a beta that lets advertisers attach backend conversion data from CRMs, order databases, and ecommerce platforms to existing tag-based conversion actions through Data Manager. The feature uses transaction IDs to deduplicate against Google tag data, recovering conversions lost to browser restrictions, privacy settings, and ad blockers, and feeding more complete signal into automated bidding.

title: Instagram now lets you add a unique caption to each carousel slide
blurb: Instagram rolled out per-slide captions for carousels, letting users write a unique caption for each image instead of one shared caption across the whole post. Combined with the earlier bump to 20 photos per carousel, the change gives social teams more room to structure multi-image stories and sequence a narrative across slides rather than front-loading everything into a single caption.

title: Your Instagram photo dumps just got a caption for every single slide
blurb: Instagram now lets creators add a unique caption to every slide in a carousel post, supporting up to 20 distinct captions per post starting today. The change turns a single carousel into a micro-blog, letting brands attach product details, tutorial steps, or context to each image rather than cramming one caption across up to 20 photos.

title: How to rank in AI search results: Expert best practices
blurb: AI search visitors convert at 4.4x the rate of standard organic traffic according to Semrush, and Ahrefs found they drove 12.1% of signups while making up just 0.5% of total traffic. With AI Overviews now appearing in roughly 48% of tracked Google searches and ChatGPT handling over a billion queries weekly, the teams building AEO citation authority now are doing it while competition is still low. The playbook starts with opening your site to AI crawlers, then structuring content around the specific prompts buyers actually ask.

title: Google launches AI agent for Ad Manager
blurb: Google launched Ask Ad Manager, a Gemini-powered conversational agent in beta this month that lets publishers troubleshoot delivery issues, generate custom reports, and navigate Google Ad Manager through plain-language prompts instead of manual report-building. Google is positioning it as the first step toward agentic ad operations, with REST APIs, an MCP server, and specialized agents for inventory discovery and deal negotiation planned through the year.

title: Google Ads brings back Target CPA and Target ROAS naming
blurb: Google Ads is restoring the standalone Target CPA and Target ROAS names this month, splitting target-based bidding strategies from volume-based ones like Maximize Conversions and Maximize Conversion Value. Bidding behavior and performance stay identical, but the cleaner labeling should make campaign setup and reporting easier to read, and API users should review integrations for the standalone TARGET_CPA and TARGET_ROAS strategy types.

title: Snowflake’s CMO Runs Marketing for 700 People. She Starts Her Day By Talking to Her Data, Not a Dashboard.
blurb: Snowflake CMO Denise Persson says she no longer opens a dashboard each morning, instead querying her marketing data in plain English to get the "why" behind pipeline moves and ending the sales-marketing fight over whose numbers are right. Her mandate for the year is 40-50% growth with flat or fewer resources, on the assumption AI absorbs the load instead of new headcount. The prerequisite she stresses: invest in your data estate first, because agents amplify bad data faster and at scale.

title: USA Today vs. Google AI Overviews: A World Cup battle for breaking news traffic
blurb: USA Today is using AI-assisted shell files to pre-write breaking sports stories and publish faster, aiming to capture search traffic before Google's AI Overviews summarize the news and absorb the click. The publisher tested the system during the 2026 Winter Olympics, where its flagship site drew 91 million page views, up 82% from 2022, and is now running five shell files a day for World Cup coverage.

title: The Mom-and-Pop SaaS era has arrived
blurb: Elena Verna argues the bigger AI shift isn't faster developers but a collapse in the cost of building software, which makes entirely new categories economically viable. As building gets cheap, the winners will be domain experts (teachers, real estate agents, accountants, coaches) shipping "Mom-and-Pop SaaS" for problems they understand deeply, rather than a small group of venture-funded engineers in a few tech hubs.

title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
blurb: Microsoft detected a self-propagating worm it calls Crypto Clipper that spreads via USB .lnk files and scans clipboards for wallet addresses and seed phrases, exfiltrating credentials and screenshots to attacker servers over a portable Tor client routed through a local SOCKS5 proxy. The malware blends data theft with remote code execution, functioning as a lightweight backdoor without traditional installers or exposed IP-based command-and-control infrastructure.

title: USB worm spreads crypto-stealing malware via Windows shortcut files
blurb: Microsoft detailed a USB worm active since February that spreads clipper malware via Windows LNK shortcut files, swapping copied wallet addresses for attacker-controlled ones and scanning clipboards every half-second for BIP39 seed phrases, Ethereum private keys, and Bitcoin and Tron addresses. The malware stages payloads from a .onion address, exfiltrates screenshots and keys over Tor, and supports remote code execution via a C2 EVAL instruction.

title: Microsoft found malware that hijacks crypto wallets and spreads through USB sticks
blurb: Microsoft identified a USB-spreading worm called Trojan:Win32/CryptoBandits that has been harvesting seed phrases, private keys, and recipient addresses from the Windows clipboard since February, exfiltrating the data over Tor. The "crypto clipper" also silently swaps in attacker-controlled wallet addresses during transfers, and Microsoft urged users to disable AutoRun and block .lnk execution on removable media.

title: U.S. agencies seek stablecoin customer-ID rules akin to banks in new GENIUS Act pitch
blurb: The Federal Reserve, Treasury, OCC, FDIC and NCUA released a proposed rule requiring stablecoin issuers to verify customer identities under Bank Secrecy Act standards, the latest step in implementing the GENIUS Act. The agencies opened a 60-day comment period, signaling that US issuers will face bank-grade KYC and sanctions-screening obligations rather than a lighter crypto-specific regime.

title: Malta's financial regulator explores bringing parts of DeFi under MiCA's orbit
blurb: Malta's Financial Services Authority opened a discussion paper exploring how DeFi could fall under the EU's MiCA framework, proposing that decentralization be assessed as a spectrum rather than a binary given that many protocols retain admin keys, concentrated governance, and upgrade rights. The MFSA is seeking feedback through July 10 on a standardized test for when a protocol sits outside MiCA's scope.

title: AWS Plugs Coinbase's x402 Into CloudFront, Letting Publishers Charge AI Agents in USDC
blurb: AWS turned on AI-traffic monetization inside AWS WAF Bot Control, letting any site behind CloudFront charge AI agents per request in USDC on Base or Solana through Coinbase's x402 protocol. A matched Monetize rule returns an HTTP 402 with a price manifest, and Coinbase's x402 Facilitator verifies the onchain payment within a single request cycle, with no new accounts, invoices, or API keys.

title: Ex-Celsius CEO Mashinsky gets U.S. CFTC ban in final resolution with regulator
blurb: The CFTC permanently banned former Celsius CEO Alexander Mashinsky from commodities registration and trading, closing its years-long fraud case without adding new fines. The order layers onto his 2025 criminal sentence of 12 years in prison, a $50,000 fine, and $48M in restitution for misleading hundreds of thousands of customers about Celsius' solvency before its 2022 collapse.

title: Algorand unveils roadmap to achieve quantum resistance by 2028
blurb: The Algorand Foundation released a roadmap to make its blockchain broadly quantum-resistant during 2027, starting in 2026 with post-quantum accounts, multisignature wallets, and staking support before extending protection to core protocol components. The plan joins similar quantum-preparedness efforts at Ethereum and Solana, all aimed at migrating away from elliptic curve cryptography well ahead of any "Q-Day" capable of breaking it.

title: Alchemy's AI-driven identity and payment service gains access to Visa network
blurb: Alchemy connected its AgentCard identity and payment service to the Visa network, letting AI agents built on any model provider transact with Visa-issued tokens while preserving rewards, credit lines, and card benefits without new credentials. Each agent also receives a dedicated email address and phone number, joining Stripe, Visa, and Mastercard in the early push into agentic commerce.

title: CLARITY Act Reaches Senate Floor With House Ready to Move Fast; Seven-Democrat Math Becomes the Gate
blurb: The Digital Asset Market Clarity Act sits on the Senate calendar eligible for a floor vote, with House Agriculture's Dusty Johnson pledging a fast companion if the Senate clears it before the August recess. Passage hinges on cloture math: Republicans' ~53 seats leave the bill roughly seven Democratic votes short, with only Gallego and Alsobrooks on record from committee and both flagging their support as contingent.

title: Backpack's Tokenized SpaceX Token on Solana Crosses 10,000 Holders, Nearly Double xStocks' SPCXx
blurb: Backpack's tokenized SpaceX share token SPCX crossed 10,000 onchain holders on Solana six days after listing, roughly double the 3,000 holders on Backed Finance's rival xStocks SPCXx. SPCX, issued by broker-dealer Backpack Securities and backed one-for-one by custodied shares redeemable via ACATS and DTCC rails, has cleared over $350M in cumulative volume since going live.

title: Live markets: Bitcoin has traded below its mining cost for five months, squeezing miners
blurb: Bitcoin has traded below its ~$78,000 production cost for five straight months, JPMorgan said, pushing roughly 20% of miners into unprofitability and forcing public miners to sell more than 32,000 BTC in Q1 to cover operating costs. Network difficulty dropped 10% in early June as higher-cost rigs powered down, and JPMorgan expects larger, more frequent adjustments while bitcoin stays near $62,500.

title: Hive shares jumps 10% on $220m Canada sovereign AI infrastructure deal
blurb: HIVE Digital Technologies shares jumped 10% in pre-market trading after the bitcoin miner signed a $220M, three-year GPU cloud contract with Bell Canada and Cohere to deploy 2,304 Nvidia Grace Blackwell GPUs for sovereign AI infrastructure in British Columbia. The deal adds roughly $70M in annual recurring revenue, pushing HIVE's contracted HPC revenue target above $100M as it pivots away from pure-play mining.

title: Schwab to join prediction markets race with S&P 500 event-based options: WSJ
blurb: Charles Schwab is partnering with Cboe Global Markets to launch binary options on the S&P 500, paying a fixed amount or nothing based on whether the index closes above a preset level, in the brokerage's first move into prediction markets. The product, expected within months, puts Schwab against Kalshi, Polymarket, Coinbase, and Robinhood, though it limits contracts to financial benchmarks and avoids politics and sports.

title: Strategy's STRC Falls to Record Low, Squeezing a Bitcoin Funding Channel
blurb: Strategy's STRC preferred stock fell to a 52-week low near $85 on Thursday, roughly 15% below its $100 stated amount and lifting the effective yield to about 13.5% against the 11.50% declared rate. The discount squeezes the at-the-market program Strategy uses to fund bitcoin buys, raising less cash per share just two weeks after the company sold BTC for the first time since 2022 to cover preferred dividends.

title: CME Group to Sue CFTC Over Perpetual Futures Approval, Citing Dodd-Frank Swaps Definition
blurb: CME Group plans to sue the CFTC over its late-May approval of crypto perpetual futures, arguing the contracts meet the Dodd-Frank definition of swaps because their funding-rate mechanism involves two parties exchanging periodic payments. A swap designation would impose a five-day margin period of risk versus one day for futures, more than doubling collateral requirements and likely pushing perpetuals offshore, as the CFTC's spokesperson called the planned suit "frivolous.

title: Sonic Labs Founders Including Andre Cronje Quit Board as New CEO Pledges to Get '1% Better' Daily
blurb: Sonic Labs' three founding board members — Andre Cronje, Michael Kong and David Richardson — resigned, with Matt Visser named CEO and Kosta Kourkoumelis named COO at the network formerly known as Fantom. The shakeup follows a roughly 97% drop in the S token from its January 2025 high and a collapse in total value locked to about $20M, down 98% from a $1.14B peak.

title: Bitcoin traders load up on bearish bets all the way down to $52,000
blurb: Bitcoin options traders piled into short- and near-dated out-of-the-money puts on Deribit over the past 24 to 48 hours, with strikes spanning $61,500 down to $52,000 across June 22 to July 31 expirations. The hedging surge tracks a hawkish Fed, persistent ETF outflows, and pressure on Strategy, whose STRC preferred stock has fallen well below its $100 par value as BTC trades near $62,400.

title: Ondo Finance Adds 173 Tokenized Stocks and ETFs, Taking Catalog Past 430 Assets Across Three Chains
blurb: Ondo Finance added 173 tokenized stocks and ETFs to Ondo Global Markets, pushing its catalog past 430 assets live simultaneously across Ethereum, Solana, and BNB Chain. The batch includes BlackRock active ETFs and covered-call income strategies, each token backed 1:1 by the underlying security held with a US broker-dealer, on a platform that crossed $1B in TVL and holds over 70% of the tokenized-equity market.

title: TD Cowen says CME has the upper hand in lawsuit against CFTC over crypto perpetual futures
blurb: CME Group sued the CFTC over its approval of perpetual futures for Kalshi and Coinbase, and TD Cowen says CME holds the stronger case on both procedural and substantive grounds. CME argues perps should be regulated as swaps rather than futures because they never expire, and TD Cowen expects it to seek a preliminary injunction blocking US perps while the case proceeds.

title: Tom Lee Says 'Zero Chance' of Ethereum Funding Crisis as Insider Warns of $30M Gap
blurb: BitMine chairman Tom Lee dismissed warnings of an Ethereum core-development funding crisis, calling the risk "zero chance" after former Foundation contributor Trent Van Epps estimated the network's 10-plus client teams and researchers cost roughly $30M a year to sustain. The dispute lands as the Ethereum Foundation deliberately shrinks its role, with co-executive director Hsiao-Wei Wang resigning amid at least eight senior departures in 2026.

title: Ethereum Foundation Loses Second Co-Executive Director as Hsiao-Wei Wang Steps Down
blurb: Hsiao-Wei Wang resigned as co-executive director of the Ethereum Foundation on Thursday, the second co-ED departure in four months and part of an exodus of at least eight senior figures since the spring. The turnover coincides with a funding warning from former coordinator Trent Van Epps, who estimates a roughly $30M annual gap for client teams and protocol-coordination groups after the Client Incentive Program expired in April.

title: U.S. agencies seek stablecoin customer-ID rules akin to banks in new GENIUS Act pitch
blurb: The Federal Reserve, Treasury, OCC, FDIC and NCUA jointly proposed a rule requiring stablecoin issuers to verify customer identities under Bank Secrecy Act standards, the latest step implementing the GENIUS Act. The proposal mandates KYC procedures, identity recordkeeping and terrorist-list screening, and opens a 60-day comment period that pulls stablecoin issuers further into the same AML compliance regime as banks and brokerages.

title: Malta's financial regulator explores bringing parts of DeFi under MiCA's orbit
blurb: Malta's MFSA published a discussion paper exploring how DeFi could fall under the EU's MiCA framework, proposing that decentralization be assessed as a spectrum rather than a binary, since most protocols retain centralized features like admin keys and governance concentration. The paper also asks whether regulated crypto firms should run smart-contract audits and governance reviews before integrating DeFi protocols, with responses open until July 10.

title: Schwab to join prediction markets race with S&P 500 event-based options: WSJ
blurb: Charles Schwab is partnering with Cboe Global Markets to offer binary options contracts on the S&P 500, paying a fixed amount or nothing based on where the index closes, in its first move into prediction markets. The brokerage is limiting the product to objectively verifiable financial outcomes rather than politics or sports, positioning it against retail-trading rivals Coinbase and Robinhood as they push deeper into the sector.

title: Ex-Celsius CEO Mashinsky gets U.S. CFTC ban in final resolution with regulator
blurb: The CFTC formally banned former Celsius CEO Alexander Mashinsky from registration and commodities trading, closing its years-long fraud case without adding new fines. The civil resolution layers onto his criminal sentence of 12 years in prison, a $50,000 fine, and $48M in restitution for misleading hundreds of thousands of customers about the safety of Celsius before its 2022 collapse.

title: Algorand unveils roadmap to achieve quantum resistance by 2028
blurb: The Algorand Foundation outlined a roadmap to make its blockchain quantum-resistant by the end of 2027, starting with post-quantum accounts, multisignature wallets, and staking support in 2026 before reworking core protocol components. The phased timeline reflects a broader industry recognition, shared by Ethereum and Solana, that migrating live cryptographic infrastructure off elliptic-curve schemes must begin years ahead of any viable quantum threat.

title: Alchemy's AI-driven identity and payment service gains access to Visa network
blurb: Alchemy connected its AgentCard identity and payment product to Visa's Intelligent Commerce network, letting AI agents built on any model transact using Visa-issued tokens while preserving rewards, credit lines, and card benefits. Each AgentCard also provisions a dedicated email address and phone number, giving agents a full identity layer to sign up for services, clear verifications, and check out without the consumer touching a payment screen.

title: AWS Plugs Coinbase's x402 Into CloudFront, Letting Publishers Charge AI Agents in USDC
blurb: AWS turned on per-request AI agent monetization inside AWS WAF Bot Control, letting any site behind CloudFront charge agents in USDC on Base or Solana through Coinbase's x402 protocol, with settlement verified onchain inside a single request cycle. It is the first time a hyperscale cloud has wired onchain settlement into its content-delivery edge, and AWS takes no cut, routing disbursement straight to the publisher's self-managed wallet.

title: CME Group to Sue CFTC Over Perpetual Futures Approval, Citing Dodd-Frank Swaps Definition
blurb: CME Group plans to sue the CFTC over its late-May approval of crypto perpetual futures, arguing the contracts are swaps under Dodd-Frank because their funding-rate mechanism involves two parties exchanging payments. A swap designation would impose a five-day margin period of risk instead of one day for futures, more than doubling collateral requirements and likely pushing perpetuals offshore, which threatens venues like Kalshi and Coinbase that the CFTC just cleared.

title: What Link data tells us about AI spending
blurb: Stripe's analysis of 250M Link wallet customers found the top 10% of AI spenders nearly doubled their monthly AI outlay from $183 in December 2025 to $359 in March 2026, with app-builder platforms like Replit and Lovable up 5x since January 2025. Stripe is tying the data to its new Link wallet for agents, which lets customers authorize AI agents to transact on their behalf with preset spending controls across any Stripe seller.

title: Backpack's Tokenized SpaceX Token on Solana Crosses 10,000 Holders, Nearly Double xStocks' SPCXx
blurb: Backpack's tokenized SpaceX share token, SPCX, crossed 10,000 onchain holders on Solana six days after listing, roughly double the 3,000 holders on rival xStocks' SPCXx. Each SPL token is backed one-for-one by a SpaceX share custodied in a Backpack Securities broker-dealer account and redeemable into a traditional brokerage via ACATS and DTCC rails, with cumulative volume passing $350M.

title: Hive shares jumps 10% on $220m Canada sovereign AI infrastructure deal
blurb: HIVE Digital Technologies signed a $220M, three-year contract with Bell Canada and Cohere to deploy 2,304 Nvidia Grace Blackwell GPUs in British Columbia, accelerating its pivot from bitcoin mining to AI compute. The deal adds roughly $70M in annual recurring revenue and pushes HIVE's contracted HPC revenue above $100M, with all infrastructure kept on Canadian soil to support Ottawa's sovereign-AI push.

title: FFinRED: An Expert-Guided Benchmark Generation and Evaluation Framework for Financial LLM Red-Teaming
blurb: This paper introduces FinRED, an expert-guided red-teaming framework for evaluating financial LLMs against finance-specific risks like regulatory evasion, fraud facilitation, and compliance violations. Its two-level taxonomy maps standards such as FATF and EU DORA to threats, and a finance-tuned rubric cut critical false negatives from 28 to 12 versus generic safety checks. FinRED is already deployed in South Korea's Financial Security Institute regulatory sandbox, with the dataset and pipeline gated to qualified researchers to limit dual-use.

title: The Transfer Market
blurb: The author dissects Wise's business of moving money, where the core challenge is a customer base that is a patchwork of niches: remittance senders, expats with overseas mortgages, cross-border workers, and small businesses, with no single coherent audience. With 19M customers and 70% acquired by word-of-mouth, Wise is now spending upwards of 5% of revenue on marketing, including a World Cup campaign aimed at the 10M visitors who will need to buy dollars.

title: Bain Capital CLO Tranche Defaults in Post-2008 First for Europe
blurb: A tranche of a European collateralized loan obligation managed by Bain Capital failed to repay investors in full, the first such default in Europe since the asset class was overhauled after 2008. The event tests confidence in CLO structures that have absorbed heavy leveraged-loan issuance, and signals that credit stress in European corporate debt is now reaching senior securitized layers once treated as effectively safe.

title: CLARITY Act Reaches Senate Floor With House Ready to Move Fast; Seven-Democrat Math Becomes the Gate
blurb: The CLARITY Act sits on the Senate calendar eligible for a floor vote, but clearing cloture requires 60 votes, leaving Republicans roughly seven Democrats short with only Gallego and Alsobrooks publicly on record, both noncommittal. The bill would assign most non-security tokens to the CFTC and route payment stablecoins to banking regulators under GENIUS, with the House ready to fast-follow if the Senate moves before August recess.

title: Sources: Bain Capital stands to make $15B+ in profits on its 2018 Kioxia buyout, a ~20x return, as Kioxia's stock has surged 5,000%+ since its December 2024 IPO (Financial Times)
blurb: Bain Capital stands to clear over $15B on its 2018 Kioxia buyout, a roughly 20x return, after the memory-chip maker's stock surged more than 5,000% since its December 2024 IPO, according to the Financial Times. The windfall ranks among the largest private-equity gains on record and underscores how the AI-driven memory boom has rewritten the math on Bain's once-troubled, debt-heavy NAND bet.

title: TD Cowen says CME has the upper hand in lawsuit against CFTC over crypto perpetual futures
blurb: CME Group sued the CFTC over its approval of crypto perpetual futures for Kalshi and Coinbase, and TD Cowen says the exchange holds the stronger case on both procedural and substantive grounds. CME argues perps, which never expire, should be regulated as swaps rather than futures, a distinction that carries higher margin requirements and worse tax treatment and could reshape how US venues list crypto derivatives.
